title: Engagement Metric for the Team/Organisation?
excerpt:
Hi everyone! 
We’ve been using Monday across our organisation for about 3 years now, and adoption has reached roughly 75% across all directorates. That said, we still have a group of colleagues who need frequent reminder…

posts.author: Ultraviolet (A. Albaajari)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!

We’ve been using Monday across our organisation for about 3 years now, and adoption has reached roughly 75% across all directorates. That said, we still have a group of colleagues who need frequent reminders or extra support to keep their data and project workflows up to date.

We have tried all manner of techniques from repeated refresher courses, sit-down sessions where we literally plug in the data together, incentives etc. We have tried appointing ‘champions’, ‘empowerment’, all the things that you hear about in the leadership/motivational courses but if some people actively work against change/adoption and usage of the tool, there’s no much else you can do. It has become increasingly frustrating as the tool is very valuable when actually utilised.

I have tried to leverage upper management support and they have asked me to come up with some kind of an ‘Engagement Metric’ that can measure who is uploading/using the tool to routinely update their plans/projects/tasks and who are not. The plan is to integrate this with their performance management.

Has anyone tried anything like this?

  • What methods worked best for you?
  • How did you design and measure your engagement metrics into something quantifiable and reportable over Q1-Q4?

Thanks for reading and I’d love to hear from others who have tackled this challenge.

posts.author: edarling85 (Wild 6 | Eric Darling)
posts.cookedHtml:
I know each board has an activity log that you can export .

We may be able to use the data to create another board and and draw up some reports

posts.author: Ultraviolet (A. Albaajari)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there and thank you for your reply. I usually check the activity log before our weekly/monthly status meetings to check whether the project manager has opened the board and updated items/tasks before our chats. Is there a way to run an automation that can check the boards for usage or frequency of activity?

posts.author: edarling85 (Wild 6 | Eric Darling)
posts.cookedHtml: Currently there is not, there is the usage stats in the admin section, but its kinda not super informative.

posts.author: anowacka (Anna from SolDevelo)
posts.cookedHtml:
I see make.com has a set of triggers you can use to create some custom reports in a tool of choice (even a spreadsheet could work in that case) - https://www.make.com/en/integrations/monday. I.e. you can use:

  • Watch Item’s Updates
  • Watch Board’s Items
  • Watch Board’s Items by Column Values (if you want to monitor changes in every column)

to monitor changes. Those triggers should include an ID of a user who made a change. Then you can use “Get a list of users” action and try to match user ID with a user name to update the spreadsheet (or other tool of your choice).

This is what I would try to do

posts.author: liambailey (Liam Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Ultraviolet I built Master Activity Logs, which acts as a ‘Master Activity Log’ for user activities across multiple boards. It offers advanced reporting based on that data as well. Thought it might be helpful for your team!

posts.author: Ultraviolet (A. Albaajari)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @liambailey , thanks so much for the suggestion. I just installed the trial app and will give it a shot
